commit.message:
Add MCP-enabled benchmark for EDAM MCP project (#321)

* Add MCP benchmark for EDAM ontology question answering

Implement a new benchmark task that evaluates models' ability to answer
questions using MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools from the EDAM ontology
server.

Features:
- New benchmark test: test_mcp_edam_qa.py evaluates MCP tool calling
  with EDAM ontology queries
- TSV to YAML conversion script: Automatically downloads and converts
  benchmark data from edammcp repository subdirectories
- MCP server integration: Pytest fixtures for connecting to local MCP
  server via stdio transport
- Support for multiple benchmark datasets: Handles recetox-aplcms and
  xcms subdirectories with unique case identifiers

Technical changes:
- Add mcp_server_config and mcp_server fixtures in conftest.py to
  manage MCP server lifecycle
- Add mcp_conversation fixture to create LangChainConversation with
  MCP tools loaded
- Fix async event loop handling in _process_manual_tool_call for MCP
  tool execution
- Update _create_tool_prompt call to include mcp parameter
- Configure MCP server to run as module with proper PYTHONPATH and
  unbuffered output

The benchmark downloads qa.tsv files from edammcp/benchmark subdirectories,
converts them to YAML format, and tests model responses against expected
EDAM ontology operation identifiers.

* adjust scoring to get score / max for mcp test

* update openai models

* Add GPT-5 models and improve MCP benchmark validation

- Add GPT-5, GPT-5-mini, and GPT-5-nano to OpenAIModels enum and base models
- Refactor model capability detection to use prefix matching helper functions
  - Introduce supports_tool_calling() and supports_structured_output() functions
  - Handle model variants with date suffixes (e.g., gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07)
  - Simplify prefix lists by removing redundant entries (base models cover variants)

- Add comprehensive validation in MCP benchmark test:
  - Pre-test: Skip models that don't support tool calling
  - Configuration: Verify MCP conversation is properly initialized
  - Post-query: Detect and fail if manual tool calling fallback is used
  - Ensures benchmark only uses native MCP binding, not manual fallback

- Update all code to use new helper functions instead of direct set lookups
- Update tests to patch helper functions instead of sets
- Add comprehensive test coverage for MCP tool calling and event loop handling

* add dev dataset for mcp benchmark

* fix: prevent double-wrapping of MCP tool arguments

Fix issue where MCP tool arguments were being double-wrapped in 'request'
object when the LLM already provided them wrapped. This caused validation
errors when calling MCP tools.

Changes:
- Check if tool_args already contains 'request' wrapper before wrapping
- Add enhanced error logging for MCP tool execution failures
- Include tool schema information in error logs for debugging

This fixes the 'description is a required property' error that occurred
when LLMs sent properly formatted MCP tool calls.

* feat: add trace logging for MCP tool calls in benchmarks

Add comprehensive trace logging to MCP EDAM QA benchmark to capture
tool call sequences, arguments, and results for debugging and analysis.

Changes:
- Add trace file format (JSONL) to capture tool schemas, calls, and results
- Integrate trace capture into test_mcp_edam_qa test
- Add view_trace.py script to easily view tool call sequences
- Capture tool schemas (what LLM sees), tool calls (what was sent),
  and tool results (what was received) for each benchmark run

Trace files are written to benchmark/results/{task}_traces.jsonl and
provide detailed insights into MCP tool calling behavior during benchmarks.

* fix pytest discovery caused by Numba / NumPy version conflict
TODO: resolve versions

* run dev benchmark

* add baseline test (same question, no MCP)

* run benchmark baseline (might be unfair)

* improve test fairness by adding system prompt with expected output

* refactor tool calling conversation to reduce redundancy

* run benchmark with better baseline

* add benchmark visualisation only for EDAM MCP tests

* formatting

* activate additional models

* add current claude

* run dev benchmark with openai and anthropic models

* prevent processing of MCP benchmarks for now

* Create the final benchmarking framework for longevity experiments (#290)

This merge includes the final updates for running the **Longevity Benchmark**.

The data analysis has been completed (`add data analysis`), and the script `Longevity_Data_Analysis.ipynb` now contains the full data evaluation, visualization, and statistical analysis.

In addition, the latest adjustments to the result files were included (`add benchmark handling adaptations` , `benchmark_utils.py`).

All requirements to be tested and the models to be evaluated were added (`add models`, `conftest.py`).

All test items adapted for **RAG** were merged with the standard items into a single file (`include rag test items in file with w/o rag items`, `benchmark_longevity_data.yaml`).

A small syntax adjustment was required for testing **RAG** items (`adjust data handling`, `load_dataset.py`).

The prompts were finalized (`finalize prompts`, `prompts.yaml`).

The test script `test_longevity_judge_responses_simultan.py` was adapted to reflect changes in the other files (`finalize test procedure`).

Previously conducted test runs were **deleted or overwritten, and the new results were generated**.

readme_html:
BioChatter
License Python
Package Build status
Tests Docker
Development Contributions

Description

🤖 BioChatter is a community-driven Python library that connects biomedical applications to conversational AI, making it easy to leverage generative AI models in the biomedical domain.

🌟 Key Features

  • Generic backend for biomedical AI applications
  • Seamless integration with multiple LLM providers
  • Native connection to BioCypher knowledge graphs
  • Extensive testing and evaluation framework
  • Living benchmark of specific biomedical applications

🚀 Demo Applications and Utilities

  • BioChatter Light - Simple Python frontend (repo)

  • BioChatter Next - Advanced Next.js frontend (repo)

  • BioChatter Server - RESTful API server

📖 Learn more in our paper (self-archived here).

Installation

To use the package, install it from PyPI, for instance using pip (pip install biochatter) or Poetry (poetry add biochatter).

Extras

The package has some optional dependencies that can be installed using the following extras (e.g. pip install biochatter[xinference]):

  • xinference: support for querying open-source LLMs through Xorbits Inference

  • podcast: support for podcast text-to-speech (for the free Google TTS; the paid OpenAI TTS can be used without this extra)

  • streamlit: support for streamlit UI functions (used in BioChatter Light)

Usage

Check out the documentation for examples, use cases, and more information. Many common functionalities covered by BioChatter can be seen in use in the BioChatter Light code base.

🤝 Getting involved

We are very happy about contributions from the community, large and small! If you would like to contribute to BioCypher development, please refer to our contribution guidelines and the developer docs. :)

If you want to ask informal questions, talk about dev things, or just chat, please join our community at https://biocypher.zulipchat.com!

Imposter syndrome disclaimer: We want your help. No, really. There may be a little voice inside your head that is telling you that you're not ready, that you aren't skilled enough to contribute. We assure you that the little voice in your head is wrong. Most importantly, there are many valuable ways to contribute besides writing code.

This disclaimer was adapted from the Pooch project.

Git LFS Configuration

This repository uses Git LFS for some large files. If you're a developer and don't need to work with these files, you have two options:

  1. Disable Git LFS smudge globally (set once for all repositories):
git lfs install --skip-smudge
git clone https://github.com/biocypher/biochatter.git
  1. Skip LFS files for a one-time clone:
GIT_LFS_SKIP_SMUDGE=1 git clone https://github.com/biocypher/biochatter.git

Both options will prevent Git LFS from downloading the large files while still allowing you to work with the repository normally.

Citation

If you use BioChatter in your work, please cite our paper.
